This enum is the entrypoint to using a certificate from a public CA for your cluster. This property was introduced to solve the
domain squatting problem with new domains. A domain name will be generated in the following format: {clustername}.{hash}.{regionname}.sfmcdomain.
The hash portion comes from Azure DNS' Deterministic Name Library. The library creates a hash using the cluster's Tenant, Subscription, Resource Group

type.doc: Defines a health policy used to evaluate the health of an application or one of its children entities.

properties.name: considerWarningAsError
properties.doc: Indicates whether warnings are treated with the same severity as errors.

properties.name: maxPercentUnhealthyDeployedApplications
properties.doc:
The maximum allowed percentage of unhealthy deployed applications. Allowed values are Byte values from zero to 100.
The percentage represents the maximum tolerated percentage of deployed applications that can be unhealthy before the application is considered in error.
This is calculated by dividing the number of unhealthy deployed applications over the number of nodes where the application is currently deployed on in the cluster.
The computation rounds up to tolerate one failure on small numbers of nodes. Default percentage is zero.

properties.name: defaultServiceTypeHealthPolicy
properties.doc: The health policy used by default to evaluate the health of a service type.

type.name: ServiceTypeHealthPolicy
type.doc: Represents the health policy used to evaluate the health of services belonging to a service type.

properties.name: maxPercentUnhealthyServices
properties.doc:
The maximum allowed percentage of unhealthy services.

The percentage represents the maximum tolerated percentage of services that can be unhealthy before the application is considered in error.
If the percentage is respected but there is at least one unhealthy service, the health is evaluated as Warning.
This is calculated by dividing the number of unhealthy services of the specific service type over the total number of services of the specific service type.
The computation rounds up to tolerate one failure on small numbers of services.

properties.name: maxPercentUnhealthyPartitionsPerService
properties.doc:
The maximum allowed percentage of unhealthy partitions per service.

The percentage represents the maximum tolerated percentage of partitions that can be unhealthy before the service is considered in error.
If the percentage is respected but there is at least one unhealthy partition, the health is evaluated as Warning.
The percentage is calculated by dividing the number of unhealthy partitions over the total number of partitions in the service.
The computation rounds up to tolerate one failure on small numbers of partitions.

properties.name: maxPercentUnhealthyReplicasPerPartition
properties.doc:
The maximum allowed percentage of unhealthy replicas per partition.

The percentage represents the maximum tolerated percentage of replicas that can be unhealthy before the partition is considered in error.
If the percentage is respected but there is at least one unhealthy replica, the health is evaluated as Warning.
The percentage is calculated by dividing the number of unhealthy replicas over the total number of replicas in the partition.
The computation rounds up to tolerate one failure on small numbers of replicas.

properties.name: serviceTypeHealthPolicyMap
properties.doc: The map with service type health policy per service type name. The map is empty by default.

properties.name: forceRestart
properties.doc: If true, then processes are forcefully restarted during upgrade even when the code version has not changed (the upgrade only changes configuration or data).

properties.name: rollingUpgradeMonitoringPolicy
properties.doc: The policy used for monitoring the application upgrade

type.name: RollingUpgradeMonitoringPolicy
type.doc: The policy used for monitoring the application upgrade

properties.name: failureAction
properties.doc: The compensating action to perform when a Monitored upgrade encounters monitoring policy or health policy violations. Invalid indicates the failure action is invalid. Rollback specifies that the upgrade will start rolling back automatically. Manual indicates that the upgrade will switch to UnmonitoredManual upgrade mode.

properties.name: healthCheckWaitDuration
properties.doc: The amount of time to wait after completing an upgrade domain before applying health policies. It is interpreted as a string representing an ISO 8601 duration with following format "hh:mm:ss.fff".

properties.name: healthCheckStableDuration
properties.doc: The amount of time that the application or cluster must remain healthy before the upgrade proceeds to the next upgrade domain. It is interpreted as a string representing an ISO 8601 duration with following format "hh:mm:ss.fff".

properties.name: healthCheckRetryTimeout
properties.doc: The amount of time to retry health evaluation when the application or cluster is unhealthy before FailureAction is executed. It is interpreted as a string representing an ISO 8601 duration with following format "hh:mm:ss.fff".

properties.name: upgradeTimeout
properties.doc: The amount of time the overall upgrade has to complete before FailureAction is executed. Cannot be larger than 12 hours. It is interpreted as a string representing an ISO 8601 duration with following format "hh:mm:ss.fff".

properties.name: upgradeDomainTimeout
properties.doc: The amount of time each upgrade domain has to complete before FailureAction is executed. Cannot be larger than 12 hours. It is interpreted as a string representing an ISO 8601 duration with following format "hh:mm:ss.fff".

properties.name: InstanceCloseDelayDurationInSeconds
properties.doc: Duration in seconds, to wait before a stateless instance is closed, to allow the active requests to drain gracefully. This would be effective when the instance is closing during the application/cluster upgrade, only for those instances which have a non-zero delay duration configured in the service description.

properties.name: upgradeMode
properties.doc: The mode used to monitor health during a rolling upgrade. The values are Monitored, and UnmonitoredAuto.

properties.name: upgradeReplicaSetCheckTimeout
properties.doc: The maximum amount of time to block processing of an upgrade domain and prevent loss of availability when there are unexpected issues. When this timeout expires, processing of the upgrade domain will proceed regardless of availability loss issues. The timeout is reset at the start of each upgrade domain. Valid values are between 0 and 42949672925 inclusive. (unsigned 32-bit integer). Unit is in seconds.

properties.name: recreateApplication
properties.doc: Determines whether the application should be recreated on update. If value=true, the rest of the upgrade policy parameters are not allowed.

properties.doc: Defines a health policy used to evaluate the health of an application or one of its children entities.

type.name: RuntimeApplicationHealthPolicy
type.doc: Cluster level definition for a health policy used to evaluate the health of an application or one of its children entities.

properties.name: considerWarningAsError
properties.doc: Indicates whether warnings are treated with the same severity as errors.

properties.name: maxPercentUnhealthyDeployedApplications
properties.doc:
The maximum allowed percentage of unhealthy deployed applications. Allowed values are Byte values from zero to 100.
The percentage represents the maximum tolerated percentage of deployed applications that can be unhealthy before the application is considered in error.
This is calculated by dividing the number of unhealthy deployed applications over the number of nodes where the application is currently deployed on in the cluster.
The computation rounds up to tolerate one failure on small numbers of nodes. Default percentage is zero.

properties.name: defaultServiceTypeHealthPolicy
properties.doc: The health policy used by default to evaluate the health of a service type.

type.name: RuntimeServiceTypeHealthPolicy
type.doc: Cluster level definition that represents the health policy used to evaluate the health of services belonging to a service type.

properties.name: maxPercentUnhealthyServices
properties.doc:
The maximum allowed percentage of unhealthy services.

The percentage represents the maximum tolerated percentage of services that can be unhealthy before the application is considered in error.
If the percentage is respected but there is at least one unhealthy service, the health is evaluated as Warning.
This is calculated by dividing the number of unhealthy services of the specific service type over the total number of services of the specific service type.
The computation rounds up to tolerate one failure on small numbers of services.

properties.name: maxPercentUnhealthyPartitionsPerService
properties.doc:
The maximum allowed percentage of unhealthy partitions per service.

The percentage represents the maximum tolerated percentage of partitions that can be unhealthy before the service is considered in error.
If the percentage is respected but there is at least one unhealthy partition, the health is evaluated as Warning.
The percentage is calculated by dividing the number of unhealthy partitions over the total number of partitions in the service.
The computation rounds up to tolerate one failure on small numbers of partitions.

properties.name: maxPercentUnhealthyReplicasPerPartition
properties.doc:
The maximum allowed percentage of unhealthy replicas per partition.

The percentage represents the maximum tolerated percentage of replicas that can be unhealthy before the partition is considered in error.
If the percentage is respected but there is at least one unhealthy replica, the health is evaluated as Warning.
The percentage is calculated by dividing the number of unhealthy replicas over the total number of replicas in the partition.
The computation rounds up to tolerate one failure on small numbers of replicas.

properties.name: serviceTypeHealthPolicyMap
properties.doc: The map with service type health policy per service type name. The map is empty by default.

properties.name: updateDescription
properties.doc: Describes the parameters for updating a rolling upgrade of application or cluster and a monitoring policy.

type.name: RuntimeRollingUpgradeUpdateMonitoringPolicy
type.doc: Describes the parameters for updating a rolling upgrade of application or cluster.

properties.name: rollingUpgradeMode
properties.doc: The mode used to monitor health during a rolling upgrade.

properties.name: forceRestart
properties.doc: If true, then processes are forcefully restarted during upgrade even when the code version has not changed (the upgrade only changes configuration or data).

properties.name: replicaSetCheckTimeoutInMilliseconds
properties.doc: The maximum amount of time to block processing of an upgrade domain and prevent loss of availability when there are unexpected issues. When this timeout expires, processing of the upgrade domain will proceed regardless of availability loss issues. The timeout is reset at the start of each upgrade domain. Valid values are between 0 and 42949672925 inclusive. (unsigned 32-bit integer).

properties.name: failureAction
properties.doc: The compensating action to perform when a Monitored upgrade encounters monitoring policy or health policy violations. Invalid indicates the failure action is invalid. Rollback specifies that the upgrade will start rolling back automatically. Manual indicates that the upgrade will switch to UnmonitoredManual upgrade mode

properties.name: healthCheckWaitDurationInMilliseconds
properties.doc: The amount of time to wait after completing an upgrade domain before applying health policies. It is first interpreted as a string representing an ISO 8601 duration. If that fails, then it is interpreted as a number representing the total number of milliseconds.

properties.name: healthCheckStableDurationInMilliseconds
properties.doc: The amount of time that the application or cluster must remain healthy before the upgrade proceeds to the next upgrade domain. It is first interpreted as a string representing an ISO 8601 duration. If that fails, then it is interpreted as a number representing the total number of milliseconds.

properties.name: healthCheckRetryTimeoutInMilliseconds
properties.doc: The amount of time to retry health evaluation when the application or cluster is unhealthy before FailureAction is executed. It is first interpreted as a string representing an ISO 8601 duration. If that fails, then it is interpreted as a number representing the total number of milliseconds.

properties.name: upgradeTimeoutInMilliseconds
properties.doc: The amount of time the overall upgrade has to complete before FailureAction is executed. It is first interpreted as a string representing an ISO 8601 duration. If that fails, then it is interpreted as a number representing the total number of milliseconds.

properties.name: upgradeDomainTimeoutInMilliseconds
properties.doc: The amount of time each upgrade domain has to complete before FailureAction is executed. It is first interpreted as a string representing an ISO 8601 duration. If that fails, then it is interpreted as a number representing the total number of milliseconds.

properties.name: instanceCloseDelayDurationInSeconds
properties.doc: Duration in seconds, to wait before a stateless instance is closed, to allow the active requests to drain gracefully. This would be effective when the instance is closing during the application/cluster upgrade, only for those instances which have a non-zero delay duration configured in the service description.

properties.name: codePackageInstanceId
properties.doc: The instance ID for currently running entry point. For a code package setup entry point (if specified) runs first and after it finishes main entry point is started. Each time entry point executable is run, its instance ID will change. If 0 is passed in as the code package instance ID, the API will restart the code package with whatever instance ID it is currently running. If an instance ID other than 0 is passed in, the API will restart the code package only if the current Instance ID matches the passed in instance ID. Note, passing in the exact instance ID (not 0) in the API is safer, because if ensures at most one restart of the code package.

properties.name: servicePackageActivationId
properties.doc: The activation id of a deployed service package. If ServicePackageActivationMode specified at the time of creating the service is 'SharedProcess' (or if it is not specified, in which case it defaults to 'SharedProcess'), then value of ServicePackageActivationId is always an empty string.

type.doc: Indicates that the ServicePlacementPolicyDescription is of type ServicePlacementInvalidDomainPolicyDescription, which indicates that a particular fault or upgrade domain cannot be used for placement of this service. The value is 0.

RequiredDomain.doc: Describes the policy to be used for placement of a Service Fabric service where the instances or replicas of that service must be placed in a particular domain.

properties.name: domainName
properties.doc: The name of the domain that should used for placement as per this policy.

properties.name: type
properties.doc: The type of placement policy for a service fabric service. Following are the possible values.

type.name: RequiredDomain
type.doc: Indicates that the ServicePlacementPolicyDescription is of type ServicePlacementRequireDomainDistributionPolicyDescription indicating that the replicas of the service must be placed in a specific domain. The value is 1.

PreferredPrimaryDomain.name: ServicePlacementPreferPrimaryDomainPolicy
PreferredPrimaryDomain.doc:
Describes the policy to be used for placement of a Service Fabric service where the service's
Primary replicas should optimally be placed in a particular domain.

This placement policy is usually used with fault domains in scenarios where the Service Fabric
cluster is geographically distributed in order to indicate that a service's primary replica should
be located in a particular fault domain, which in geo-distributed scenarios usually aligns with regional
or datacenter boundaries. Note that since this is an optimization it is possible that the Primary replica
may not end up located in this domain due to failures, capacity limits, or other constraints.

properties.name: domainName
properties.doc: The name of the domain that should used for placement as per this policy.

properties.name: type
properties.doc: The type of placement policy for a service fabric service. Following are the possible values.

type.name: PreferredPrimaryDomain
type.doc: Indicates that the ServicePlacementPolicyDescription is of type ServicePlacementPreferPrimaryDomainPolicyDescription, which indicates that if possible the Primary replica for the partitions of the service should be located in a particular domain as an optimization. The value is 2.

RequiredDomainDistribution.name: ServicePlacementRequireDomainDistributionPolicy
RequiredDomainDistribution.doc:
Describes the policy to be used for placement of a Service Fabric service where two replicas
from the same partition should never be placed in the same fault or upgrade domain.

While this is not common it can expose the service to an increased risk of concurrent failures
due to unplanned outages or other cases of subsequent/concurrent failures. As an example, consider
a case where replicas are deployed across different data center, with one replica per location.
In the event that one of the datacenters goes offline, normally the replica that was placed in that
datacenter will be packed into one of the remaining datacenters. If this is not desirable then this
policy should be set.

properties.name: domainName
properties.doc: The name of the domain that should used for placement as per this policy.

properties.name: type
properties.doc: The type of placement policy for a service fabric service. Following are the possible values.

type.name: RequiredDomainDistribution
type.doc: Indicates that the ServicePlacementPolicyDescription is of type ServicePlacementRequireDomainDistributionPolicyDescription, indicating that the system will disallow placement of any two replicas from the same partition in the same domain at any time. The value is 3.

NonPartiallyPlaceService.name: ServicePlacementNonPartiallyPlaceServicePolicy
NonPartiallyPlaceService.doc: The type of placement policy for a service fabric service. Following are the possible values.

type.name: NonPartiallyPlaceService
type.doc: Indicates that the ServicePlacementPolicyDescription is of type ServicePlacementNonPartiallyPlaceServicePolicyDescription, which indicates that if possible all replicas of a particular partition of the service should be placed atomically. The value is 4.

properties.name: healthPolicy
properties.doc: The cluster health policy defines a health policy used to evaluate the health of the cluster during a cluster upgrade.

type.name: ManagedClusterHealthPolicy
type.doc: Defines a health policy used to evaluate the health of the cluster or of a cluster node.

properties.name: maxPercentUnhealthyNodes
properties.doc:
The maximum allowed percentage of unhealthy nodes before reporting an error. For example, to allow 10% of nodes to be unhealthy, this value would be 10.

The percentage represents the maximum tolerated percentage of nodes that can be unhealthy before the cluster is considered in error.
If the percentage is respected but there is at least one unhealthy node, the health is evaluated as Warning.
The percentage is calculated by dividing the number of unhealthy nodes over the total number of nodes in the cluster.
The computation rounds up to tolerate one failure on small numbers of nodes. Default percentage is zero.

In large clusters, some nodes will always be down or out for repairs, so this percentage should be configured to tolerate that.

properties.name: maxPercentUnhealthyApplications
properties.doc:
The maximum allowed percentage of unhealthy applications before reporting an error. For example, to allow 10% of applications to be unhealthy, this value would be 10.

The percentage represents the maximum tolerated percentage of applications that can be unhealthy before the cluster is considered in error.
If the percentage is respected but there is at least one unhealthy application, the health is evaluated as Warning.
This is calculated by dividing the number of unhealthy applications over the total number of application instances in the cluster, excluding applications of application types that are included in the ApplicationTypeHealthPolicyMap.
The computation rounds up to tolerate one failure on small numbers of applications. Default percentage is zero.

properties.name: deltaHealthPolicy
properties.doc: The cluster delta health policy defines a health policy used to evaluate the health of the cluster during a cluster upgrade.

type.name: ManagedClusterUpgradeDeltaHealthPolicy
type.doc: Describes the delta health policies for the cluster upgrade.

properties.name: maxPercentDeltaUnhealthyNodes
properties.doc:
The maximum allowed percentage of nodes health degradation allowed during cluster upgrades.
The delta is measured between the state of the nodes at the beginning of upgrade and the state of the nodes at the time of the health evaluation.
The check is performed after every upgrade domain upgrade completion to make sure the global state of the cluster is within tolerated limits.

properties.name: maxPercentUpgradeDomainDeltaUnhealthyNodes
properties.doc:
The maximum allowed percentage of upgrade domain nodes health degradation allowed during cluster upgrades.
The delta is measured between the state of the upgrade domain nodes at the beginning of upgrade and the state of the upgrade domain nodes at the time of the health evaluation.
The check is performed after every upgrade domain upgrade completion for all completed upgrade domains to make sure the state of the upgrade domains is within tolerated limits.

properties.name: maxPercentDeltaUnhealthyApplications
properties.doc:
The maximum allowed percentage of applications health degradation allowed during cluster upgrades.
The delta is measured between the state of the applications at the beginning of upgrade and the state of the applications at the time of the health evaluation.
The check is performed after every upgrade domain upgrade completion to make sure the global state of the cluster is within tolerated limits. System services are not included in this.
NOTE: This value will overwrite the value specified in properties.UpgradeDescription.HealthPolicy.MaxPercentUnhealthyApplications

properties.name: monitoringPolicy
properties.doc: The cluster monitoring policy describes the parameters for monitoring an upgrade in Monitored mode.

type.name: ManagedClusterMonitoringPolicy
type.doc: Describes the monitoring policies for the cluster upgrade.

properties.name: healthCheckWaitDuration
properties.doc: The length of time to wait after completing an upgrade domain before performing health checks. The duration can be in either hh:mm:ss or in d.hh:mm:ss.ms format.

properties.name: healthCheckStableDuration
properties.doc: The amount of time that the application or cluster must remain healthy before the upgrade proceeds to the next upgrade domain. The duration can be in either hh:mm:ss or in d.hh:mm:ss.ms format.

properties.name: healthCheckRetryTimeout
properties.doc: The amount of time to retry health evaluation when the application or cluster is unhealthy before the upgrade rolls back. The timeout can be in either hh:mm:ss or in d.hh:mm:ss.ms format.

properties.name: upgradeTimeout
properties.doc: The amount of time the overall upgrade has to complete before the upgrade rolls back. The timeout can be in either hh:mm:ss or in d.hh:mm:ss.ms format.

properties.name: upgradeDomainTimeout
properties.doc: The amount of time each upgrade domain has to complete before the upgrade rolls back. The timeout can be in either hh:mm:ss or in d.hh:mm:ss.ms format.

properties.name: upgradeReplicaSetCheckTimeout
properties.doc:
The maximum amount of time to block processing of an upgrade domain and prevent loss of availability when there are unexpected issues.
When this timeout expires, processing of the upgrade domain will proceed regardless of availability loss issues.
The timeout is reset at the start of each upgrade domain. The timeout can be in either hh:mm:ss or in d.hh:mm:ss.ms format.
This value must be between 00:00:00 and 49710.06:28:15 (unsigned 32 bit integer for seconds)

properties.doc: The port used for token-auth based HTTPS connections to the cluster. Cannot be set to the same port as HttpGatewayEndpoint.

properties.name: IsHttpGatewayExclusiveAuthModeEnabled
properties.doc: If true, token-based authentication is not allowed on the HttpGatewayEndpoint. This is required to support TLS versions 1.3 and above. If token-based authentication is used, HttpGatewayTokenAuthConnectionPort must be defined.

properties.name: enableOutboundOnlyNodeTypes

properties.name: skipManagedNsgAssignment

HTTP/1.1 uses entity tags in the etag (section 14.19), If-Match (section 14.24), If-None-Match (section 14.26), and If-Range (section 14.27) header fields.",

properties.name: loadBalancerBackendAddressPoolId
properties.doc: The resource Id of the Load Balancer backend address pool that the VM instances of the node type are associated with. The format of the resource Id is '/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/Microsoft.Network/loadBalancers/{loadBalancerName}/backendAddressPools/{backendAddressPoolName}'.

properties.name: loadBalancerInboundNatPoolId
properties.doc: The resource Id of the Load Balancer inbound NAT pool that the VM instances of the node type are associated with. The format of the resource Id is '/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/Microsoft.Network/loadBalancers/{loadBalancerName}/inboundNatPools/{inboundNatPoolName}'.

properties.name: applicationGatewayBackendAddressPoolId
properties.doc: The resource Id of application gateway backend address pool. The format of the resource Id is '/subscriptions/<subscriptionId>/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/Microsoft.Network/applicationGateways/{applicationGatewayName}/backendAddressPools/{backendAddressPoolName}'.

properties.name: networkSecurityRules
properties.doc: The Network Security Rules for this node type. This setting can only be specified for node types that are configured with frontend configurations.

properties.name: additionalDataDisks
properties.doc: Additional managed data disks.

valueType.name: NodeTypeVmssDataDisk
valueType.doc: Managed data disk description.

properties.name: lun
properties.doc: Specifies the logical unit number of the data disk. This value is used to identify data disks within the VM and therefore must be unique for each data disk attached to a VM. Lun 0 is reserved for the service fabric data disk.

properties.name: DiskSizeInGB
properties.doc: Disk size for each vm in the node type in GBs.

properties.name: diskType
properties.doc: Managed data disk type. Specifies the storage account type for the managed disk

properties.name: diskLetter
properties.doc: Managed data disk letter. It can not use the reserved letter C or D and it can not change after created.

properties.name: IsEncryptionAtHostEnabled
properties.doc: Enable or disable the Host Encryption for the virtual machines on the node type. This will enable the encryption for all the disks including Resource/Temp disk at host itself. Default: The Encryption at host will be disabled unless this property is set to true for the resource.

properties.name: provisioningState
properties.doc: The provisioning state of the node type resource.

properties.name: IsAcceleratedNetworkingEnabled
properties.doc: Specifies whether the network interface is accelerated networking-enabled.

properties.name: useDefaultPublicLoadBalancer
properties.doc: Specifies whether the use public load balancer. If not specified and the node type doesn't have its own frontend configuration, it will be attached to the default load balancer. If the node type uses its own Load balancer and useDefaultPublicLoadBalancer is true, then the frontend has to be an Internal Load Balancer. If the node type uses its own Load balancer and useDefaultPublicLoadBalancer is false or not set, then the custom load balancer must include a public load balancer to provide outbound connectivity.

properties.name: useTempDataDisk
properties.doc: Specifies whether to use the temporary disk for the service fabric data root, in which case no managed data disk will be attached and the temporary disk will be used. It is only allowed for stateless node types.

properties.name: IsOverProvisioningEnabled
properties.doc: Specifies whether the node type should be overprovisioned. It is only allowed for stateless node types.

properties.name: zones
properties.doc: Specifies the availability zones where the node type would span across. If the cluster is not spanning across availability zones, initiates az migration for the cluster.

properties.name: IsSpotVm
properties.doc: Indicates whether the node type will be Spot Virtual Machines. Azure will allocate the VMs if there is capacity available and the VMs can be evicted at any time.

properties.name: hostGroupId
properties.doc: Specifies the full host group resource Id. This property is used for deploying on azure dedicated hosts.

properties.name: useEphemeralOSDisk
properties.doc: Indicates whether to use ephemeral os disk. The sku selected on the vmSize property needs to support this feature.

properties.name: spotRestoreTimeout
properties.doc: Indicates the time duration after which the platform will not try to restore the VMSS SPOT instances specified as ISO 8601.

properties.name: evictionPolicy
properties.doc: Specifies the eviction policy for virtual machines in a SPOT node type. Default is Delete.

properties.name: vmImageResourceId
properties.doc: Indicates the resource id of the vm image. This parameter is used for custom vm image.

properties.name: subnetId
properties.doc: Indicates the resource id of the subnet for the node type.

properties.name: vmSetupActions
properties.doc: Specifies the actions to be performed on the vms before bootstrapping the service fabric runtime.

properties.name: securityType
properties.doc: Specifies the security type of the nodeType. Supported values include Standard, TrustedLaunch and ConfidentialVM.

properties.name: securityEncryptionType
properties.doc: Specifies the EncryptionType of the managed disk. It is set to DiskWithVMGuestState for encryption of the managed disk along with VMGuestState blob and VMGuestStateOnly for encryption of just the VMGuestState blob. Note: It can be set for only Confidential VMs.

properties.name: IsSecureBootEnabled
properties.doc: Specifies whether secure boot should be enabled on the nodeType. Can only be used with TrustedLaunch and ConfidentialVM SecurityType.

properties.name: IsNodePublicIPEnabled
properties.doc: Specifies whether each node is allocated its own public IPv4 address. This is only supported on secondary node types with custom Load Balancers.

properties.name: IsNodePublicIPv6Enabled
properties.doc: Specifies whether each node is allocated its own public IPv6 address. This is only supported on secondary node types with custom Load Balancers.

properties.name: vmSharedGalleryImageId
properties.doc: Indicates the resource id of the vm shared galleries image. This parameter is used for custom vm image.

properties.name: natGatewayId
properties.doc: Specifies the resource id of a NAT Gateway to attach to the subnet of this node type. Node type must use custom load balancer.

properties.name: natConfigurations
properties.doc: Specifies the NAT configuration on default public Load Balancer for the node type. This is only supported for node types use the default public Load Balancer.

valueType.name: NodeTypeNatConfig
valueType.doc: Provides information about NAT configuration on the default public Load Balancer for the node type.

properties.name: backendPort
properties.doc: The internal port for the NAT configuration.

properties.name: frontendPortRangeStart
properties.doc: The port range start for the external endpoint.

properties.name: frontendPortRangeEnd
properties.doc: The port range end for the external endpoint.

properties.name: vmImagePlan
properties.doc: Specifies information about the marketplace image used to create the virtual machine. This element is only used for marketplace images. Before you can use a marketplace image from an API, you must enable the image for programmatic use. In the Azure portal, find the marketplace image that you want to use and then click Want to deploy programmatically, Get Started ->. Enter any required information and then click Save.

type.name: VmImagePlan
type.doc: Specifies information about the marketplace image used to create the virtual machine. This element is only used for marketplace images. Before you can use a marketplace image from an API, you must enable the image for programmatic use. In the Azure portal, find the marketplace image that you want to use and then click Want to deploy programmatically, Get Started. Enter any required information and then click Save.

properties.name: name
properties.doc: The plan ID.

properties.name: product
properties.doc: Specifies the product of the image from the marketplace. This is the same value as Offer under the imageReference element.

properties.name: promotionCode
properties.doc: The promotion code.

properties.name: publisher
properties.doc: The publisher ID.

properties.name: serviceArtifactReferenceId
properties.doc: Specifies the service artifact reference id used to set same image version for all virtual machines in the scale set when using 'latest' image version.

properties.name: dscpConfigurationId
properties.doc: Specifies the resource id of the DSCP configuration to apply to the node type network interface.

properties.name: additionalNetworkInterfaceConfigurations
properties.doc: Specifies the settings for any additional secondary network interfaces to attach to the node type.

valueType.name: AdditionalNetworkInterfaceConfiguration
valueType.doc: Specifies the settings for a network interface to attach to the node type.

properties.name: name
properties.doc: Name of the network interface.

properties.name: enableAcceleratedNetworking
properties.doc: Specifies whether the network interface is accelerated networking-enabled.

properties.name: dscpConfiguration
properties.doc: Specifies the DSCP configuration to apply to the network interface.

type.name: SubResource
type.doc: Azure resource identifier.

properties.name: id
properties.doc: Azure resource identifier.

properties.name: IPConfigurations
properties.doc: Specifies the IP configurations of the network interface.

valueType.name: ServiceFabricManagedClusterIPConfiguration
valueType.doc: Specifies an IP configuration of the network interface.

properties.name: name
properties.doc: Name of the network interface.

properties.name: applicationGatewayBackendAddressPools
properties.doc: Specifies an array of references to backend address pools of application gateways. A node type can reference backend address pools of multiple application gateways. Multiple node types cannot use the same application gateway.

properties.name: loadBalancerBackendAddressPools
properties.doc: Specifies an array of references to backend address pools of load balancers. A node type can reference backend address pools of one public and one internal load balancer. Multiple node types cannot use the same basic sku load balancer.

properties.name: loadBalancerInboundNatPools
properties.doc: Specifies an array of references to inbound Nat pools of the load balancers. A node type can reference inbound nat pools of one public and one internal load balancer. Multiple node types cannot use the same basic sku load balancer.

properties.name: subnet
properties.doc: Specifies the subnet of the network interface.

properties.name: privateIPAddressVersion
properties.doc: Specifies whether the IP configuration's private IP is IPv4 or IPv6. Default is IPv4.

properties.name: publicIPAddressConfiguration
properties.doc: The public IP address configuration of the network interface.

type.name: ServiceFabricManagedClusterPublicIPAddressConfiguration
type.doc: The public IP address configuration of the network interface.

properties.name: name
properties.doc: Name of the network interface.

properties.name: IPTags
properties.doc: Specifies the list of IP tags associated with the public IP address.

properties.name: publicIPAddressVersion
properties.doc: Specifies whether the IP configuration's public IP is IPv4 or IPv6. Default is IPv4.

properties.name: computerNamePrefix
properties.doc: Specifies the computer name prefix. Limited to 9 characters. If specified, allows for a longer name to be specified for the node type name.

properties.name: vmApplications
properties.doc: Specifies the gallery applications that should be made available to the underlying VMSS.

valueType.name: ServiceFabricManagedVmApplication
valueType.doc: Specifies the gallery application that should be made available to the underlying VMSS.

properties.name: configurationReference
properties.doc: Optional, Specifies the uri to an azure blob that will replace the default configuration for the package if provided.

properties.name: enableAutomaticUpgrade
properties.doc: If set to true, when a new Gallery Application version is available in PIR/SIG, it will be automatically updated for the underlying VMSS.

properties.name: order
properties.doc: Optional, Specifies the order in which the packages have to be installed.

properties.name: packageReferenceId
properties.doc: Specifies the GalleryApplicationVersion resource id on the form of /subscriptions/{SubscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{ResourceGroupName}/providers/Microsoft.Compute/galleries/{galleryName}/applications/{application}/versions/{version}.

properties.name: vmGalleryTags
properties.doc: Optional, Specifies a passthrough value for more generic context. Accepts a JSON-formatted string e.g. '{"Tag1":"Value1","Tag2":"Value2"}'.

properties.name: treatFailureAsDeploymentFailure
properties.doc: Optional, If true, any failure for any operation in the VmApplication will fail the deployment.

properties.name: IsZoneBalanceEnabled
properties.doc: Setting this to true allows stateless node types to scale out without equal distribution across zones.

properties.name: isOutboundOnly
properties.doc: Specifies the node type should be configured for only outbound traffic and not inbound traffic.

properties.name: enableResilientEphemeralOsDisk
properties.doc: Specifies whether the node type should use a resilient ephemeral OS disk when using a supported SKU size. A resilient ephemeral OS disk provides improved reliability for ephemeral OS disks by enabling full caching.

properties.name: name
properties.doc: The name of the node type.

properties.name: tags
properties.doc: Resource tags.

properties.name: sku
properties.doc: The node type sku.

type.name: NodeTypeSku
type.doc: Describes a node type sku.

properties.name: name
properties.doc: The sku name. Name is internally generated and is used in auto-scale scenarios. Property does not allow to be changed to other values than generated. To avoid deployment errors please omit the property.

properties.name: tier
properties.doc: Specifies the tier of the node type. Possible Values: **Standard**

properties.name: capacity
properties.doc: The number of nodes in the node type. If present in request it will override properties.vmInstanceCount.

models.doc: Node type update request

models.name: NodeTypeActionContent

properties.name: nodes
properties.doc: List of node names from the node type.

properties.doc: The supported SKU for a for node type.

type.doc: Describes a node type supported sku.

properties.name: tier

properties.name: capacity
properties.doc: Provides information about how the node count can be scaled.
